hypothesis: An equivariant graph neural network trained on the XDIP dataset to predict Fe K-edge XANES spectra directly from 3D local atomic coordinates will achieve significantly lower prediction error than invariant GNN architectures (GCN, GAT, GIN) because the rotational and translational symmetries of iron coordination environments are physically meaningful features that invariant pooling operations destroy, and this improvement will be most pronounced for iron sites with low-symmetry coordination geometries (e.g., distorted octahedral or square pyramidal) where angular information is diagnostically critical for distinguishing spectral pre-edge features.
